behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: This is a deterministic code-path concern: does the module construct the API request correctly? A mock that captures and verifies the request arguments is sufficient. The failure (omitted labels) occurs at the code logic boundary, not at the API or infrastructure layer. If labels are missing from the request, the test's assertion on the mock call arguments will fail.
behaviors.test_description: Mock the external API with a spy that captures call arguments. Invoke the classification module. Assert the mock was called and the arguments include all labels from the taxonomy. This catches the failure without needing real API interaction.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns 'unknown' instead of forwarding the unexpected value.
behaviors.justification: This is error-handling logic: the module must validate API responses and substitute invalid values. A mock that returns invalid labels, combined with an assertion that the output is 'unknown', directly tests this code path. The failure (forwarding invalid labels) is caught by logic, not infrastructure.
behaviors.test_description: Mock the API to return an invalid label (e.g., 'fraud' when taxonomy is ['legal', 'technical', 'financial', 'other']). Call the classification module. Assert the returned label is 'unknown'. Test multiple invalid values to ensure comprehensive handling.

behaviors.description: The classification result for the same document is the same across repeated calls.
behaviors.justification: For an LLM API (non-deterministic), repeated calls without caching would produce different results. To test caching, use a non-deterministic mock that returns different values on each call—this simulates the real LLM's non-determinism. Call the module twice with the same document; if caching works, results are identical; if missing, results differ, causing test failure. The mock must be stateful to simulate this.
behaviors.test_description: Mock the API to return different classification results on each invocation (simulating LLM non-determinism, e.g., 'legal' then 'technical' for the same document). Call the classification function twice with identical input. Assert both results are equal. If caching is not implemented, the second call returns a different result and the test fails.

behaviors.description: The number of tokens consumed for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is an observational constraint on real API behavior. Token counting is implementation-specific to the external LLM API (GPT, Claude, Llama, etc. have different tokenizers and counting rules). The failure—'actual tokens exceed budget'—only manifests with the real API. A mock can return any token count, but that doesn't verify the real API's consumption. Only a real API call reveals whether actual token consumption respects the budget.
behaviors.test_description: Call the classification module with a typical document from the target domain. Capture the token count reported by the real API. Assert that the actual token consumption is within the configured per-call budget. Repeat with several document lengths to establish the pattern. This directly verifies the real observational constraint.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: The failure—returning an error instead of retrying—is a logic-layer issue. The retry behavior itself is testable via mocking: mock the API to return a rate-limit signal on the first call and success on the second; mock the sleep/wait function to avoid actual delays. If the module doesn't implement retry logic, it returns the error from the first call, and the test fails. The core behavior (retry upon rate limit) is deterministic and fully testable with mocks.
behaviors.test_description: Mock the external API to return a rate-limit error on the first call and a successful classification result on the second call. Mock the sleep/wait function. Invoke the classification module. Assert it returns a successful classification (not an error), implying the retry succeeded. Verify the sleep function was called, confirming the module waited between attempts.
